title: connect_with_failover_increments_counter makes real network call
description: wallet/src/client.rs unit test connect_with_failover_increments_counter: the test calls connect_with_failover(&["https://127.0.0.1:1"]) which makes a real TCP connection attempt to localhost port 1. While connection-refused is nearly instant, this is a live network syscall in a unit test. On systems where port 1 is bound, or in sandboxed CI environments with blocked outbound connections that produce different error behavior (e.g. EHOSTUNREACH vs ECONNREFUSED), the test behavior may differ from intent. The test's only goal is to observe the counter increment, not to exercise the connection path. A cleaner approach: read the counter before and after connect_with_failover(&[]) (the empty-list path), which errors immediately without any network call. However, the empty-list path returns before incrementing the counter (current code bails before fetch_add), so the test would need to use a single-entry list. Using a clearly-invalid URL like "https://invalid.invalid:1" that produces an immediate parse or DNS error without a TCP attempt would be preferable.
close_reason: Already resolved: connect_with_failover_increments_counter was replaced with connect_with_failover_exhausts_all_endpoints (behavioral test, no network syscall needed)
